name: Gillig Low Floor (29-foot)
funFact: Known for its 'kneeling' feature, this bus can lower its front suspension to the curb to allow easier boarding for passengers with limited mobility.

name: New Flyer Xcelsior XDE40
funFact: The Series-E HybriDrive system allows the diesel engine to shut down completely while stopped or at low speeds to drastically reduce emissions.

name: Van Hool Exqui.City (Articulated)
funFact: Often called a 'streetcar on rubber tires,' these articulated giants use optical guidance systems to help the driver dock perfectly at stations.

name: Siemens Viaggio Comfort
funFact: These train cars feature pressure-sealed cabins so that passengers' ears don't 'pop' when entering tunnels at high speeds.

name: Alstom Euroduplex (TGV)
funFact: This is the world's only double-decker high-speed train, designed to move massive crowds across Europe at triple-digit speeds.

name: JR Central L0 Series (Maglev)
funFact: This train levitates 4 inches above the track using superconducting magnets, holding the world land speed record for rail at 375 mph.

name: Embraer E190-E2
funFact: Nicknamed the 'Profit Hunter,' its oversized engines make it the most fuel-efficient single-aisle aircraft in the regional market.

name: Airbus A321neo
funFact: The engine fans on this jet are 81 inches wide—significantly wider than the entire cabin diameter of most small private jets.

name: Airbus A380-800
funFact: The 'Queen of the Skies' is so large that many global airports had to undergo multi-million dollar renovations just to accommodate its wingspan at the gate.

name: Nova Bus LFSe+ Rapid
funFact: This battery-electric bus can recharge through overhead pantograph bursts at route endpoints instead of sitting idle for long depot charges.

name: Alexander Dennis Enviro500
funFact: Double-decker coaches like this one move huge suburban crowds while still fitting inside the footprint of a standard single bus bay.

name: MCI D45 CRT LE
funFact: Built for long-haul commuter duty, this low-entry coach blends highway range with easier boarding than traditional intercity buses.

name: Stadler FLIRT H2
funFact: Instead of a diesel engine, this train carries hydrogen tanks and fuel cells, producing only water vapor at the tailpipe.

name: Siemens Charger Venture
funFact: The Venture trainset uses semi-permanently coupled coaches, cutting dwell time and making corridor service feel more like a fast metro than classic rail.

name: Alstom Avelia Liberty
funFact: Its articulated design shares wheelsets between cars, reducing drag and making very high-speed trips smoother through curves.

name: De Havilland Dash 8-400
funFact: The Q400 is a turboprop, but it cruises so fast that it can match jet block times on many short regional routes.

name: Boeing 737 MAX 8
funFact: Its winglets stand taller than a one-story house and recover enough efficiency to save airlines millions in fuel over a fleet's lifetime.

name: Airbus A220-300
funFact: Passengers love the A220's oversized windows and huge overhead bins, features that make a small jet feel unexpectedly roomy.

name: Boeing 787-9 Dreamliner
funFact: Its composite fuselage lets cabin pressure stay closer to sea level, which is why travelers often step off long Dreamliner flights feeling less worn out.

name: New Flyer DE60LFA (RapidRide Prototype)
funFact: King County Metro's unit 6000 was a RapidRide prototype with a rear window, a feature dropped from the later production coaches.

name: Orion VII Hybrid
funFact: These were the first Metro buses equipped with series diesel-electric hybrid propulsion.

name: New Flyer Xcelsior XDE35
funFact: This order was transferred to New Flyer after the Orion factory closed, and its Series-E system shuts the diesel engine off while stopped.

name: New Flyer Xcelsior XT40
funFact: Metro's XT40 fleet was its first low-floor trolleybus line with air conditioning and batteries for short off-wire operation.

name: New Flyer Xcelsior XT60
funFact: This articulated trolleybus combines wire power with battery flexibility, giving Metro a modern 60-foot electric workhorse.

name: New Flyer Xcelsior XDE60 (RapidRide G)
funFact: Metro's RapidRide G buses have five doors, including two on the left side, plus interior bike racks.

name: New Flyer Xcelsior XE40
funFact: Metro's first XE40s entered service in 2022 after an inspection and testing program that began with unit 4700.

name: New Flyer Xcelsior XE60
funFact: Metro's XE60 carries a 525 kWh battery pack and was advertised with a roughly 140-mile range.

name: Gillig Low Floor EV Plus
funFact: This became part of Metro's 2024-2025 battery-electric expansion, with the first buses entering service in early 2026.

name: Solaris Urbino 12 Electric
funFact: King County Metro became the first North American buyer of a Solaris bus with its Urbino 12 Electric order.

name: Solaris Urbino 18 Electric
funFact: This 60-foot Solaris order gave Metro an unusually distinctive articulated electric bus option for the fleet.

name: Breda DuoBus 350
funFact: Seattle's Breda DuoBus fleet could run as both a diesel bus and an electric trolleybus, making it one of Metro's most unusual vehicle types.

name: MAN SG-T 310
funFact: These were part of the first fleet of articulated trolleybuses to operate in the United States.

name: Twin Coach GWFT
funFact: Originally built in 1940, this preserved Seattle trolleybus was restored to operating condition and later returned to its original fleet number, 905.

name: Proterra Catalyst BE40
funFact: Metro's Proterra battery buses were retired unusually early after parts availability became a problem across multiple agencies.

name: Acela Express
funFact: Each original Acela trainset used electric power cars permanently coupled to a set of six trailer cars, making it Amtrak's first modern high-speed flagship.

name: Talgo Series 8
funFact: Amtrak Cascades uses these lightweight articulated trainsets, with Oregon owning two distinctive Series 8 sets.

name: Siemens Venture (Gold Runner)
funFact: This California configuration is a seven-car Venture trainset with a cab car, designed to run with separate diesel-electric locomotives.

name: Amtrak Airo Cascades
funFact: The Cascades Airo set pairs Venture-style coaches with new Charger-family motive power for a six-car corridor trainset.

name: Amtrak Airo Northeast Regional
funFact: This Airo variant uses a power car to pull electricity from overhead lines and feed the trainset's traction motors.

name: Empire Builder Superliner Set
funFact: A full Empire Builder consist typically relies on two ALC-42 locomotives hauling a long string of Superliners across some of the most remote intercity territory in the country.

name: Sounder BiLevel Set
funFact: A Sounder set combines an EMD F59PHI with seven Bombardier BiLevel coaches, giving it massive commuter seating compared with typical intercity trainsets.
